{\n475: \t\tif (a.charCodeAt(aLastIndex - i) !== b.charCodeAt(bLastIndex - i)) {\n476: \t\t\treturn i;\n477: \t\t}\n478: \t}\n479: \n480: \treturn len;\n481: }\n482: \n483: /**\n484:  * See /**\n491:  * See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surrogate_pair\n492:  */\n493: export function isLowSurrogate(charCode: number): boolean {\n494: \treturn (0xDC00 <= charCode && charCode <= CharCode.A + 1);\n916: \n917: \tn = n % (2 * LETTERS_CNT);\n918: \n919: \tif (n < LETTERS_CNT) {\n920: \t\treturn String.fromCharCode(CharCode.a + n);\n921: \t}\n922: \n923: \treturn String.fromCharCode(CharCode.A + n - LETTERS_CNT);\n924: }\n925: \n926: //#region Unicode Grapheme Break\n927: \n928: export function getGraphemeBreakType(codePoint: number): GraphemeBreakType {\n929: \tconst graphemeBreakTree = GraphemeBreakTree.getInstance();\n930: \treturn graphemeBreakTree.getGraphemeBreakType(codePoint);\n931: }\n932: \n933: function breakBetweenGraphemeBreakType(breakTypeA: GraphemeBreakType, breakTypeB: GraphemeBreakType): boolean {\n934: \t// http://www.unicode.org/reports/tr29/#Grapheme_Cluster_Boundary_Rules\n935: \n936: \t// !!! Let's make the common case a bit faster\n937: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.Other) {\n938: \t\t// see https://www.unicode.org/Public/13.0.0/ucd/auxiliary/GraphemeBreakTest-13.0.0d10.html#table\n939: \t\treturn (breakTypeB !== GraphemeBreakType.Extend && breakTypeB !== GraphemeBreakType.SpacingMark);\n940: \t}\n941: \n942: \t// Do not break between a CR and LF. Otherwise, break before and after controls.\n943: \t// GB3                                        CR × LF\n944: \t// GB4                       (Control | CR | LF) ÷\n945: \t// GB5                                           ÷ (Control | CR | LF)\n946: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.CR) {\n947: \t\tif (breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.LF) {\n948: \t\t\treturn false; // GB3\n949: \t\t}\n950: \t}\n951: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.Control || breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.CR || breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.LF) {\n952: \t\treturn true; // GB4\n953: \t}\n954: \tif (breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.Control || breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.CR || breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.LF) {\n955: \t\treturn true; // GB5\n956: \t}\n957: \n958: \t// Do not break Hangul syllable sequences.\n959: \t// GB6                                         L × (L | V | LV | LVT)\n960: \t// GB7                                  (LV | V) × (V | T)\n961: \t// GB8                                 (LVT | T) × T\n962: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.L) {\n963: \t\tif (breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.L || breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.V || breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.LV || breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.LVT) {\n964: \t\t\treturn false; // GB6\n965: \t\t}\n966: \t}\n967: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.LV || breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.V) {\n968: \t\tif (breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.V || breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.T) {\n969: \t\t\treturn false; // GB7\n970: \t\t}\n971: \t}\n972: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.LVT || breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.T) {\n973: \t\tif (breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.T) {\n974: \t\t\treturn false; // GB8\n975: \t\t}\n976: \t}\n977: \n978: \t// Do not break before extending characters or ZWJ.\n979: \t// GB9                                           × (Extend | ZWJ)\n980: \tif (breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.Extend || breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.ZWJ) {\n981: \t\treturn false; // GB9\n982: \t}\n983: \n984: \t// The GB9a and GB9b rules only apply to extended grapheme clusters:\n985: \t// Do not break before SpacingMarks, or after Prepend characters.\n986: \t// GB9a                                          × SpacingMark\n987: \t// GB9b                                  Prepend ×\n988: \tif (breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.SpacingMark) {\n989: \t\treturn false; // GB9a\n990: \t}\n991: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.Prepend) {\n992: \t\treturn false; // GB9b\n993: \t}\n994: \n995: \t// Do not break within emoji modifier sequences or emoji zwj sequences.\n996: \t// GB11    \\p{Extended_Pictographic} Extend* ZWJ × \\p{Extended_Pictographic}\n997: \tif (breakTypeA === GraphemeBreakType.ZWJ && breakTypeB === GraphemeBreakType.Extended_Pictographic) {\n998: \t\t// Note: we are not implementing the rule entirely here to avoid introducing states\n999: \t\treturn false; // GB12 & GB13\n1007: \t}\n1008: \n1009: \t// GB999                                     Any ÷ Any\n1010: \treturn true;\n1011: }\n1012: \n1013: export const enum GraphemeBreakType {\n1014: \tOther = 0,\n1015: \tPrepend = 1,\n1016: \tCR = 2,\n1017: \tLF = 3,\n1018: \tControl = 4,\n1019: \tExtend = 5,\n1020: \tRegional_Indicator = 6,\n1021: \tSpacingMark = 7,\n1022: \tL = 8,\n1023: \tV = 9,\n1024: \tT = 10,\n1025: \tLV = 11,\n1026: \tLVT = 12,\n1027: \tZWJ = 13,\n1028: \tExtended_Pictographic = 14\n1029: }\n1030: \n1031: class GraphemeBreakTree {\n1032: \n1033: \tprivate static _INSTANCE: GraphemeBreakTree | null = null;\n1034: \tpublic static getInstance(): GraphemeBreakTree {\n1035: \t\tif (!GraphemeBreakTree._INSTANCE) {\n1036: \t\t\tGraphemeBreakTree._INSTANCE = new GraphemeBreakTree();\n1037: \t\t}\n1038: \t\treturn GraphemeBreakTree._INSTANCE;\n1039: \t}\n1040: \n1041: \tprivate readonly _data: number[];\n1042: \n1043: \tconstructor() {\n1044: \t\tthis._data = getGraphemeBreakRawData();\n1045: \t}\n1046: \n1047: \tpublic getGraphemeBreakType(codePoint: number): GraphemeBreakType {\n1048: \t\t// !!! Let's make 7bit ASCII a bit faster: 0..31\n1049: \t\tif (codePoint < 32) {\n1050: \t\t\tif (codePoint === CharCode.LineFeed) {\n1051: \t\t\t\treturn GraphemeBreakType.LF;\n1052: \t\t\t}\n1053: \t\t\tif (codePoint === CharCode.CarriageReturn) {\n1054: \t\t\t\treturn GraphemeBreakType.CR;\n1055: \t\t\t}\n1056: \t\t\treturn GraphemeBreakType.Control;\n1057: \t\t}\n1058: \t\t// !!! Let's make 7bit ASCII a bit faster: 32..126\n1059: \t\tif (codePoint < 127) {\n1060: \t\t\treturn GraphemeBreakType.Other;\n1061: \t\t}\n1062: \n1063: \t\tconst data = this._data;\n1064: \t\tconst nodeCount = data.length / 3;\n1065: \t\tlet nodeIndex = 1;\n1066: \t\twhile (nodeIndex <= nodeCount) {\n1067: \t\t\tif (codePoint < data[3 * nodeIndex]) {\n1068: \t\t\t\t// go left\n1069: \t\t\t\t// hit\n1075: \t\t\t\treturn data[3 * nodeIndex + 2];\n1076: \t\t\t}\n1077: \t\t}\n1078: \n1079: \t\treturn GraphemeBreakType.Other;\n1080: \t}\n1081: }\n1082: \n1083: function \t}\n126: \n127: \tpublic findLineFeedCountBeforeOffset(offset: number): number {\n128: \t\tconst lineFeedsOffsets = this._lineFeedsOffsets;\n129: \t\tlet min = 0;\n130: \t\tlet max = before first line feed\n139: \t\t\treturn 0;\n140: \t\t}\n141: \n142: \t\twhile (min < max) {\n143: \t\t\tconst mid = min + ((max - min) / 2 >> 0);\n144: \n145: \t\t\tif (lineFeedsOffsets[mid] >= offset) {\n146: \t\t\t\tmax = mid - 1;\n147: \t\t\t} else {\n148: \t\t\t\tif (lineFeedsOffsets[mid + 1] >= offset) {\n149: \t\t\t\t\t// bingo!\n150: \t\t\t\t\tmin = mid;\n151: \t\t\t\t\tmax = mid;\n152: \t\t\t\t} else {\n153: \t\t\t\t\tmin = mid + 1;\n154: \t\t\t\t}\n155: \t\t\t}\n156: \t\t}\n157: \t\treturn min + 1;\n158: \t}\n159: }\n160: \n161: export class TextModelSearch {\n162: \n163: \tpublic static findMatches(model: TextModel, searchParams: SearchParams, searchRange: Range, captureMatches: boolean, limitResultCount: number): FindMatch[] {\n164: \t\tconst searchData = searchParams.parseSearchRequest();\n165: \t\tif (!searchData) {\n166: \t\t\treturn [];\n167: \t\t}\n168: \n169: \t\tif (searchData.regex.multiline) {\n170: \t\t\treturn this._doFindMatchesMultiline(model, searchRange, new Searcher(searchData.wordSeparators, searchData.regex), captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n171: \t\t}\n172: \t\treturn this._doFindMatchesLineByLine(model, searchRange, searchData, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n173: \t}\n174: \n175: \t/**\n176: \t * Multiline search always executes on the lines concatenated with \\n.\n177: \t * We must therefore compensate for the count of \\n in case the model is CRLF\n178: \t */\n179: \tprivate static _getMultilineMatchRange(model: TextModel, deltaOffset: number, text: string, lfCounter: LineFeedCounter | null, matchIndex: number, match0: string): Range {\n180: \t\tlet startOffset: number;\n181: \t\tlet lineFeedCountBeforeMatch = 0;\n182: \t\tif (lfCounter) {\n183: \t\t\tlineFeedCountBeforeMatch = lfCounter.findLineFeedCountBeforeOffset(matchIndex);\n184: \t\t\tstartOffset = deltaOffset + matchIndex + lineFeedCountBeforeMatch /* add as many \\r as there were \\n */;\n185: \t\t} else {\n186: \t\t\tstartOffset = deltaOffset + matchIndex;\n187: \t\t}\n188: \n189: \t\tlet endOffset: number;\n190: \t\tif (lfCounter) {\n191: \t\t\tconst lineFeedCountBeforeEndOfMatch = lfCounter.findLineFeedCountBeforeOffset(matchIndex + match0.length);\n192: \t\t\tconst lineFeedCountInMatch = lineFeedCountBeforeEndOfMatch - lineFeedCountBeforeMatch;\n193: \t\t\tendOffset = startOffset + match0.length + lineFeedCountInMatch /* add as many \\r as there were \\n */;\n194: \t\t} else {\n195: \t\t\tendOffset = startOffset + match0.length;\n196: \t\t}\n197: \n198: \t\tconst startPosition = model.getPositionAt(startOffset);\n199: \t\tconst endPosition = model.getPositionAt(endOffset);\n200: \t\treturn new Range(startPosition.lineNumber, startPosition.column, endPosition.lineNumber, endPosition.column);\n201: \t}\n202: \n203: \tprivate static _doFindMatchesMultiline(model: TextModel, searchRange: Range, searcher: Searcher, captureMatches: boolean, limitResultCount: number): FindMatch[] {\n204: \t\tconst deltaOffset = model.getOffsetAt(searchRange.getStartPosition());\n205: \t\t// We always execute multiline search over the lines joined with \\n\n206: \t\t// This makes it that \\n will match the EOL for both CRLF and LF models\n207: \t\t// We compensate for offset errors in `_getMultilineMatchRange`\n208: \t\tconst text = model.getValueInRange(searchRange, EndOfLinePreference.LF);\n209: \t\tconst lfCounter = (model.getEOL() === '\\r\\n' ? new LineFeedCounter(text) : null);\n210: \n211: \t\tconst result: FindMatch[] = [];\n212: \t\tlet counter = 0;\n213: \n214: \t\tlet m: RegExpExecArray | null;\n215: \t\tsearcher.reset(0);\n216: \t\twhile ((m = searcher.next(text))) {\n217: \t\t\tresult[counter++] = createFindMatch(this._getMultilineMatchRange(model, deltaOffset, text, lfCounter, m.index, m[0]), m, captureMatches);\n218: \t\t\tif (counter >= limitResultCount) {\n219: \t\t\t\treturn result;\n220: \t\t\t}\n221: \t\t}\n222: \n223: \t\treturn result;\n224: \t}\n225: \n226: \tprivate static _doFindMatchesLineByLine(model: TextModel, searchRange: Range, searchData: SearchData, captureMatches: boolean, limitResultCount: number): FindMatch[] {\n227: \t\tconst result: FindMatch[] = [];\n228: \t\tlet resultLen = 0;\n229: \n230: \t\t// Early case for a search range that starts & stops on the same line number\n231: \t\tif (searchRange.startLineNumber === searchRange.endLineNumber) {\n232: \t\t\tconst text = model.getLineContent(searchRange.startLineNumber).substring(searchRange.startColumn - 1, searchRange.endColumn - 1);\n233: \t\t\tresultLen = this._findMatchesInLine(searchData, text, searchRange.startLineNumber, searchRange.startColumn - 1, resultLen, result, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n234: \t\t\treturn result;\n235: \t\t}\n236: \n237: \t\t// Collect results from first line\n238: \t\tconst text = model.getLineContent(searchRange.startLineNumber).substring(searchRange.startColumn - 1);\n239: \t\tresultLen = this._findMatchesInLine(searchData, text, searchRange.startLineNumber, searchRange.startColumn - 1, resultLen, result, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n240: \n241: \t\t// Collect results from middle lines\n242: \t\tfor (let lineNumber = searchRange.startLineNumber + 1; lineNumber < searchRange.endLineNumber && resultLen < limitResultCount; lineNumber++) {\n243: \t\t\tresultLen = this._findMatchesInLine(searchData, model.getLineContent(lineNumber), lineNumber, 0, resultLen, result, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n244: \t\t}\n245: \n246: \t\t// Collect results from last line\n247: \t\tif (resultLen < limitResultCount) {\n248: \t\t\tconst text = model.getLineContent(searchRange.endLineNumber).substring(0, searchRange.endColumn - 1);\n249: \t\t\tresultLen = this._findMatchesInLine(searchData, text, searchRange.endLineNumber, 0, resultLen, result, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n250: \t\t}\n251: \n252: \t\treturn result;\n253: \t}\n254: \n255: \tprivate static _findMatchesInLine(searchData: SearchData, text: string, lineNumber: number, deltaOffset: number, resultLen: number, result: FindMatch[], captureMatches: boolean, limitResultCount: number): number {\n256: \t\tconst wordSeparators = Let me systematically look for defects in each file.\n\n## 1. `src/vs/base/common/strings.ts` - `createRegExp` function\n\nLines 226-228:\n```typescript\nif (!options.matchCase) {\n    modifiers += 'i';\n}\n```\n\nThis is **INVERTED** logic. The `i` flag makes the regex case-**insensitive**. So when `matchCase` is `true` (we want case-sensitive search), we should NOT add `i`. When `matchCase` is `false`, we WANT case-insensitive search, so we SHOULD add `i`.\n\n\nActually, wait—let me reconsider. When `matchCase` is false, we want case-insensitive matching, so adding the `i` flag is correct. When `matchCase` is true, we want case-sensitive matching, so we don't add `i`. The logic checks out.\n\nNow let me look at the `wholeWord` condition...\n\nThe logic checks if characters at the start and end of the search string are word characters using `!/\\B/.test()`. Since `\\B` matches non-word-boundaries, this test returns true when we're at an actual word boundary, so we add `\\b` anchors appropriately. That looks right.\n\nLet me check the `isMultilineRegexSource` function next. I'm realizing the logic here might be off—`\\W` matches non-word characters and could theoretically span lines, but that's not really what makes a regex multiline. The actual multiline indicators should be `\\n`, `\\r`, and possibly `\\s` since whitespace includes newlines. So checking for `CharCode.W` alongside the escape sequences seems questionable unless there's a specific reason `\\W` patterns need special handling.\n\nNow I'm looking at a different issue in `languageFeatureRegistry.ts` with the `_compareByScoreAndTime` method. I'm verifying the sort comparator logic for the entries. The score comparison is working correctly—higher scores come first. Use when you need to interact with the VS Code UI, automate the chat panel, test UI features, or take screenshots of VS Code. Triggers include 'automate VS Code', 'interact with chat', 'test the UI', 'take a screenshot', 'launch Code OSS with debugging'.\\\"\\n+metadata:\\n+  allowed-tools: Bash(agent-browser:*), Bash(npx agent-browser:*)\\n+---\\n+\\n+# VS Code Automation\\n+\\n+Automate VS Code (Code OSS) using agent-browser. VS Code is built on Electron/Chromium and exposes a Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) port that agent-browser can connect to, enabling the same snapshot-interact workflow used for web pages.\\n+\\n+## Prerequisites\\n+\\n+- **`agent-browser` must be installed.** It's listed in devDependencies — run `npm install` in the repo root. Use `npx agent-browser` if it's not on your PATH, or install globally with `npm install -g agent-browser`.\\n+- **For Code OSS (VS Code dev build):** The repo must be built before launching. `./scripts/code.sh` runs the build automatically if needed, or set `VSCODE_SKIP_PRELAUNCH=1` to skip the compile step if you've already built.\\n+- **CSS selectors are internal implementation details.** Selectors like `.interactive-input-part`, `.interactive-input-editor`, and `.part.auxiliarybar` used in `eval` commands are VS Code internals that may change across versions. If they stop working, use `agent-browser snapshot -i` to re-discover the current DOM structure.\\n+\\n+## Core Workflow\\n+\\n+1. **Launch** Code OSS with remote debugging enabled\\n+2. **Connect** agent-browser to the CDP port\\n+3. **Snapshot** to discover interactive elements\\n+4. **Interact** using element refs\\n+5. **Re-snapshot** after navigation or state changes\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+# Launch Code OSS with remote debugging\\n+./scripts/code.sh --remote-debugging-port=9224\\n+\\n+# Wait for Code OSS to start, retry until connected\\n+for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do agent-browser connect 9224 2>/dev/null && break || sleep 3; done\\n+\\n+# Discover UI elements\\n+agent-browser snapshot -i\\n+\\n+# Focus the chat input (macOS)\\n+agent-browser press Control+Meta+i\\n+```\\n+\\n+## Connecting\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+# Connect to a specific port\\n+agent-browser connect 9222\\n+\\n+# Or use --cdp on each command\\n+agent-browser --cdp 9222 snapshot -i\\n+\\n+# Auto-discover a running Chromium-based app\\n+agent-browser --auto-connect snapshot -i\\n+```\\n+\\n+After `connect`, all subsequent commands target the connected app without needing `--cdp`.\\n+\\n+## Tab Management\\n+\\n+Electron apps often have multiple windows or webviews. Use tab commands to list and switch between them:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+# List all available targets (windows, webviews, etc.)\\n+agent-browser tab\\n+\\n+# Switch to a specific tab by index\\n+agent-browser tab 2\\n+\\n+# Switch by URL pattern\\n+agent-browser tab --url \\\"*settings*\\\"\\n+```\\n+\\n+## Launching Code OSS (VS Code Dev Build)\\n+\\n+The VS Code repository includes `scripts/code.sh` which launches Code OSS from source. It passes all arg\\n...
